title: Optimization Using Prefix Array

content.problemStatement:
Given an array of length nnn, we need to perform mmm query over the array.

In each query you need to find the sum of the element in the range aaa to bbb.

Without using prefix sum
For each query we need to iterate over the length of array, which will take O(n)O(n)O(n) time.
Total m query will make the time complexity O(n∗m)O(n*m)O(n∗m)
Using prefix sum
Create an prefix sum array in O(n)O(n)O(n) time.
For each query we can find the sum of a range in O(1)O(1)O(1) time, using prefix[b]−prefix[a−1]prefix[b] - prefix[a-1]prefix[b]−prefix[a−1] as the sum of range from aaa to bbb
Total mmm query will make the time complexity m∗O(1)+O(n)m * O(1) + O(n)m∗O(1)+O(n) => O(m)+O(n)O(m) + O(n)O(m)+O(n)
We can see above clearly how prefix sum help us to optimize the time complexity of our code.

TaskUse the optimized algorithm to solve the problem.


Input Format

First line contains an integer NNN, the length of the array.
Second line contains NNN elements a1, a2, a3 . . . . arNNN.
Third line gives an integer KKK, the number of queries.
Next KKK line contains queries with integer aiii and biii.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:- A line with integer N, the length of the array.- A line with N space‑separated integers a1 … aN.- A line with integer K, the number of queries.- K lines each containing two integers ai and bi (1‑based indices) describing a query for the sum of the sub‑array from ai to bi inclusive. If ai > bi the range should be considered after swapping them.
content.outputFormat: For each query of each test case output a single line containing the sum of the elements in the requested range.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case sums: 1‑3=60, 2‑2=20. Second test case array of four 5s gives sums 1‑4=20, 2‑3=10, 4‑4=5.
Only one element, the query asks for the whole array.

solution.approach: Build a prefix sum array pref where pref[i] = sum of first i elements (1‑based). Then the sum of any sub‑array [l, r] is pref[r] - pref[l-1]. For each query we possibly swap l and r to guarantee l <= r and answer in O(1). The prefix array is built in O(N) per test case, so total complexity is O(N + K) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N + K) per test case, O(total N + total K) for the whole input.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) per test case for the prefix array.

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # i: test case index from 1 to 20
    # RINT(a,b): random integer in [a,b]
    # POWER(a,b): a**b
    if i == 1:
        print(1)
        print(1)
        print(1)
        print(1)
        print('1 1')
    elif i == 2:
        print(1)
        print(1)
        print(100000)
        print(1)
        print('1 1')
